category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: A rack-back three-branch fixture tree. A grounded box spine carries upper, middle, and lower branch arms on separate side saddles, and the three tips end in a pad, fork, and clamp tab. Each branch rotates on its own revolute hub axis with no shared linkage between the branches.

category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: A tower-mounted three-branch rotary stand. A heavy foot and short tower carry three radial arms on distinct collar blocks, with the arm lengths intentionally uneven so the silhouette stays legible. The three arms are independent revolute joints about separate supported axes.
label: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches Rebalanced to 1500 2

category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: A ladder-frame three-branch inspection tree. A narrow central frame supports three stub arms on cheek blocks at staggered heights, and each arm remains a single rigid member rather than a sub-linkage. Each branch is its own revolute joint about a local supported hub axis.

category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: A pedestal three-branch tooling tree. A round pedestal carries three side-supported rotary branches with different reach lengths and compact rectangular end faces. The branches are independent revolute joints about separate supported axes on the pedestal.

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: A fork-backed two-branch tooling tree. A narrow backbone carries two rotary side arms on distinct cheek plates, with the upper arm ending in a fork and the lower arm ending in a tab. The two side arms are separate revolute joints about their own supported hubs.

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: A pedestal two-branch positioning stand. A compact pedestal supports two branch modules at staggered heights so the motion envelopes stay visually separate. The branches are independent revolute joints about distinct supported axes.

prompt: A nested slide pair with end plate. A grounded outer guide carries a middle stage, and the middle stage carries a compact output stage. The extension uses two serial prismatic joints.
label: Two-stage telescoping slide Rebalanced to 1500 3

prompt: A drawer-slide style two-stage extension. A fixed outer channel carries a large moving member, and the large member carries a smaller output member with a plain face. The moving members are serial prismatic joints.
label: Two-stage telescoping slide Rebalanced to 1500 4

prompt: A double-box telescoping slide. A grounded outer section carries a middle box member, and the middle member carries a slim inner member. The slide uses two serial prismatic joints along a shared axis.
label: Two-stage telescoping slide Rebalanced to 1500 5

prompt: A nested runner two-stage extension. A fixed outer section supports a middle runner and a carried output runner with clear overlap between all sections. The two runners are serial prismatic joints.
label: Two-stage telescoping slide Rebalanced to 1500 6

prompt: A framed louver bank with independent vanes. A grounded rectangular frame supports a row of rigid slats with visible end pivots and credible slat thickness. Each slat is its own revolute joint about its long pivot axis.
label: Vane array with independent pivots Rebalanced to 1500 1

prompt: A inspection shutter array with separate vanes. A fixed outer frame carries a bank of parallel rigid vanes with simple rectangular cross sections and visible pivot pins at the ends. Each vane rotates on an independent revolute joint about its long axis.
label: Vane array with independent pivots Rebalanced to 1500 2

prompt: A boxed frame vane array. A grounded rectangular housing supports several evenly spaced rigid vanes, each carried on its own pair of end supports. The vanes are independent revolute joints about their long supported axes.
label: Vane array with independent pivots Rebalanced to 1500 3

prompt: A louvers-on-frame pivot bank. A fixed frame carries a row of thick slats with clear spacing between them and simple end pivots at each side. Each slat is a separate revolute joint about its long pivot axis.
label: Vane array with independent pivots Rebalanced to 1500 4

prompt: A rectangular frame airflow vane set. A grounded frame supports a bank of rigid rectangular vanes with visible side pivots and a simple, uncoupled layout. Each vane rotates independently on its own revolute axis.
label: Vane array with independent pivots Rebalanced to 1500 5

prompt: A panel-frame slat array. A fixed outer frame carries a row of rigid slats with simple edge thickness and obvious pivot supports at the ends. The slats are independent revolute joints about their long axes.
label: Vane array with independent pivots Rebalanced to 1500 6

prompt: A mast-and-wrist slide. A grounded vertical guide carries a moving carriage, and the carriage carries a compact hinged output face at its front. The carriage is a vertical prismatic joint and the output face is a carried revolute joint.
label: Vertical slide with wrist hinge Rebalanced to 1500 1

prompt: A upright lift slide with wrist plate. A fixed mast supports a moving carriage, and the carriage supports a short hinged plate with a plain front face. The carriage moves on a vertical prismatic joint, and the plate rotates on a supported revolute hinge.
label: Vertical slide with wrist hinge Rebalanced to 1500 2

prompt: A vertical carriage with tilting nose. A grounded upright guide carries a moving block, and the block carries a compact hinged nose link. The block is a vertical prismatic joint, and the nose link is a carried revolute joint.
label: Vertical slide with wrist hinge Rebalanced to 1500 3

prompt: A column slide with output hinge. A fixed column carries a sliding carriage, and the carriage carries a short hinged output pad between side cheeks. The carriage is a vertical prismatic joint and the output pad is a revolute joint at the carriage face.
label: Vertical slide with wrist hinge Rebalanced to 1500 4

prompt: A stacked XYZ positioning stage. A grounded X base carries a Y carriage, and the Y carriage carries a vertical Z slide with a compact top table. The X and Y axes are orthogonal prismatic joints, and the Z axis is a vertical prismatic joint.
label: XYZ Cartesian stage Rebalanced to 1500 1

prompt: A three-axis cartesian transfer stage. A fixed base rail supports an X carriage, the X carriage supports a cross Y slide, and the Y slide supports a short vertical head. The mechanism uses two orthogonal horizontal prismatic joints and one vertical prismatic joint.
label: XYZ Cartesian stage Rebalanced to 1500 2

prompt: A machine-table XYZ slide stack. A grounded lower X guide carries a broad carriage, the carriage carries a Y slide, and the Y slide carries a vertical Z stage. The X, Y, and Z motions are three supported prismatic joints.
label: XYZ Cartesian stage Rebalanced to 1500 3

prompt: A inspection XYZ gantry-lite stage. A fixed base carries a long X slide, the X slide carries a transverse Y runner, and the Y runner carries a compact vertical slide. The three axes are orthogonal supported prismatic joints.
label: XYZ Cartesian stage Rebalanced to 1500 4

prompt: A cross-slide XYZ module. A grounded lower slide carries a middle cross-slide, and the middle cross-slide carries a short vertical stage ending in a plain faceplate. The lower, middle, and upper stages are prismatic joints along X, Y, and Z.
label: XYZ Cartesian stage Rebalanced to 1500 5

prompt: A box-rail XYZ transfer head. A fixed base guide supports an X carriage, the X carriage supports a sideways Y slide, and the Y slide supports a vertical output stage. The three supported motions are prismatic joints along orthogonal X, Y, and Z axes.
label: XYZ Cartesian stage Rebalanced to 1500 6

prompt: A turntable-and-fork yaw-pitch head. A grounded lower stage carries a fork, and the fork supports a compact pitch cradle between its arms. The lower stage yaws about a vertical revolute axis, and the cradle pitches about a horizontal revolute axis.
label: Yaw-pitch module Rebalanced to 1500 1

prompt: A pedestal yaw-pitch inspection head. A fixed pedestal supports a compact yaw stage, and the yaw stage carries a side-supported pitch frame with a plain output face. The module uses a vertical yaw revolute joint and a horizontal pitch revolute joint.
label: Yaw-pitch module Rebalanced to 1500 2

prompt: A pan-base pitch cradle module. A grounded rotary base carries a forked pitch support with a compact head between the side cheeks. The base yaws on a revolute joint about vertical, and the carried head pitches on a revolute trunnion axis.
label: Yaw-pitch module Rebalanced to 1500 3

prompt: A tower yaw-pitch sensor head. A fixed tower carries a compact yaw stage and a pitch yoke centered above it with a small front faceplate. The yaw stage rotates on a vertical revolute axis, and the yoke carries a horizontal pitch revolute joint.
label: Yaw-pitch module Rebalanced to 1500 4

prompt: A ring-base yaw-pitch head. A grounded ring base carries a compact fork cradle with a short carried head between the arms. The base provides a vertical yaw revolute joint, and the carried head provides a horizontal pitch revolute joint.
label: Yaw-pitch module Rebalanced to 1500 5

prompt: A cartridge-and-yoke yaw-pitch-roll wrist. A grounded base supports a yaw stage, the yaw stage supports a pitch cradle, and the pitch cradle supports a compact roll cartridge. The wrist uses three serial revolute joints: yaw about vertical, pitch about a cross-axis, and roll about the tool axis.
label: Yaw-pitch-roll wrist Rebalanced to 1500 1

prompt: A pedestal yaw-pitch-roll head. A fixed pedestal carries a compact yaw module, a side-supported pitch yoke, and a short roll spindle at the center of the yoke. The module uses serial yaw, pitch, and roll revolute joints on supported axes.
label: Yaw-pitch-roll wrist Rebalanced to 1500 2

prompt: A turntable-yoke-spindle wrist. A grounded lower yaw stage carries a pitch frame, and the pitch frame carries a centered roll spindle with a plain faceplate. The stage uses a vertical yaw revolute joint, a horizontal pitch revolute joint, and a roll revolute joint about the spindle axis.
label: Yaw-pitch-roll wrist Rebalanced to 1500 3

prompt: A tower wrist with roll cartridge. A fixed support tower carries a yaw stage, a compact pitch cradle, and a short roll cartridge nested inside the cradle. Yaw, pitch, and roll are three serial revolute joints on supported axes.
label: Yaw-pitch-roll wrist Rebalanced to 1500 4
